source_pr.title: planner, executor: support aggregation as inner side of index join
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Issue Number: close #37068

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

Documentation

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.


symptoms: select /*+ INL_JOIN(tmp) */ t1.a, tmp.count_b from (select a, count(b) count_b from t group by a) tmp, t1 where tmp.a=t1.a order by t1.a, tmp.count_b; (tests/integrationtest/r/planner/core/indexjoin.result)
failure_signature: select /*+ INL_JOIN(tmp) */ t1.a, tmp.count_b from (select a, count(b) count_b from t group by a) tmp, t1 where tmp.a=t1.a order by t1.a, tmp.count_b; (tests/integrationtest/r/planner/core/indexjoin.result)
root_cause_explanation: Insufficient evidence to attribute this PR to a specific flaky-test root cause; the diff primarily adds support for using aggregation as the inner side of an index join and updates the integration test expectations accordingly.
fix_pattern: Support aggregation as the inner side of index join and update `indexjoin` integration test expectations.

evidence: evidence: select /*+ INL_JOIN(tmp) */ t1.a, tmp.count_b from (select a, count(b) count_b from t group by a) tmp, t1 where tmp.a=t1.a order by t1.a, tmp.count_b; (tests/integrationtest/r/planner/core/indexjoin.result)
